claims.text: An unidentified object known as the Black Knight satellite has been orbiting Earth for over a hundred years and, according to a decoded message in its radio transmissions, has been watching humanity for thirteen thousand years.

evidence.quote:
for over 100 years an unidentified
object orbiting the earth has been seen
heard and photographed by people all
around the world this object became
known as the black knight satellite
the signals from the black knight
satellite have been decoded
and if the translation is correct it's
been watching us for 13 000 years

claims.rationale: This is the host's framing thesis for the entire video. The 'Black Knight satellite' as a single object with a 13,000-year history is the modern internet legend the host stitches together — the actual photographic evidence (STS-88) is officially a thermal blanket and the radio decoding (Lunan) was retracted by its author. The host himself walks back the strongest version of this thesis in his verdict; directTruth set very low because the umbrella claim has no supporting evidence.

claims.text: In 1899 at his Colorado Springs lab, Nikola Tesla received a periodic numerical radio transmission that he believed originated from outer space and represented evidence of intelligent life on another planet.

evidence.quote:
1899 tesla got
a surprise his tower started receiving a
transmission a transmission that tesla
believed to be artificial his antenna
was receiving numbers at a regular
interval at first tesla thought it was a
signal coming from high in the
atmosphere it turned out the signal was
coming from higher than that it was
coming from space
tesla believed that this was evidence of
life on anoth

claims.rationale: Tesla did report receiving anomalous repeating radio signals at Colorado Springs around 1899-1900 and publicly speculated they could be from intelligent extraterrestrial origin (e.g. his Collier's Weekly piece 'Talking with the Planets', 1901). The historical claim of his belief is well-attested. The 'numbers at a regular interval' framing is somewhat embellished — Tesla described regularity but not numerical encoding per se. Modern consensus, as the host himself notes later, is these were natural emissions (likely Jovian noise / pulsars).

claims.text: On May 14, 1954 — three years before Sputnik — newspapers reported that Marine Corps Major Donald Keyhoe, citing US government scientists at White Sands, claimed one or two artificial satellites were already circling the Earth and being tracked.
entities: organization:national investigations committee on aerial phenomena or nycap

evidence.quote:
lso caused
the pentagon to panic
[Music]
on may 14 1954 three years before
sputnik became the first satellite in
orbit a strange news story appeared the
report claimed that according to an
expert one or two artificial satellites
are circling the earth the expert in
question was marine corps major donald
kehoe major kehoe was a naval aviator
who later became a ufo researcher and
founded the national investigations
committee on aerial phenomena or nycap
he claimed that u.s government
scientists working out of white sands
new mexico had found one or two
artificial satellites and we're trying
to track them a few weeks later we got
some more details i

claims.rationale: There were genuine 1954 newspaper reports (e.g. San Francisco Examiner, May 1954) attributed to Donald Keyhoe describing alleged satellite tracking at White Sands, and Keyhoe is a verifiable historical figure who later founded NICAP. However, modern reconstruction shows the original AP wire was based on speculative remarks by astronomer Clyde Tombaugh's earth-satellite search, and the specifics about confirmed artificial satellites were never substantiated. Reporting existed; what was reported was unreliable.

claims.text: On February 11, 1960 the New York Times reported that the U.S. Defense Department had publicly acknowledged tracking an unidentified silent satellite in near-polar orbit, dubbed the 'dark satellite', whose identity and origin remained unknown after nearly two weeks of observation.

evidence.quote:
60 the defense
department finally acknowledged it on
february 11 1960 the new york times ran
an article which said washington
february 10 an unidentified silent
satellite has been discovered circling
the earth in a near polar orbit by
united states tracking stations the
defense department said today

claims.rationale: The Feb 11 1960 NYT article is verifiable and quoted accurately in spirit. The DoD did briefly track an anomalous object in February 1960, which was later officially identified — as the host concedes in his verdict — as the lost Discoverer V (Corona) capsule's spent Agena stage that had drifted into an unexpected orbit. The 'mystery satellite acknowledged' framing is historically accurate; the unresolved-mystery framing is contested.

claims.text: From his Paris lab in 1961, French astronomer Jacques Vallée observed, tracked, and filmed the Black Knight satellite — finding it to be in polar orbit and roughly the size of an oil truck weighing 15 tons — but his photographs and film were confiscated and destroyed by his observatory under the pretext of institutional embarrassment.

evidence.quote:
paris lab
jacques valet observed tracked and even
filmed the black knight satellite the
first thing that stood out was that it
was in polar orbit now there were no
earth-based satellites in polar orbit
yet what's interesting about this is
satellites in polar orbit are very well
positioned to observe all points of the
earth's surface wh

supervisor
the materials were immediately
confiscated the photos were destroyed
and the film was erased the official
reason for destroying the film was that
the observatory was embarrassed that it
couldn't identify the object now that's
a bunch o

claims.rationale: Jacques Vallée is a real and well-known French astronomer-turned-UFO-researcher who has publicly discussed early 1960s tracking work that produced data later destroyed. The specific claims of polar orbit, 15 tons, oil-truck size, and confiscation come almost entirely from Vallée's own later memoir accounts — there is no independent corroboration, no surviving material, and no contemporaneous publication. The story is on-record but not independently verified.

claims.text: During Mercury 9 in 1963, astronaut Gordon Cooper reported seeing a green glowing object ahead of his module — a sighting confirmed by ground crew and a tracking station — but after the mission NASA suppressed the story by attributing it to CO2-induced hallucinations from an electronic malfunction.

evidence.quote:
ury 9 cooper reported seeing a green
glowing object ahead of his module his
ground crew also confirmed this and a
tracking station saw it moving east to
west
prograde nb

but when cooper got back from
the mission the press was not allowed to
ask about the sighting the official
explanation was there was an electronic
malfunction on cooper's craft this
caused high levels of carbon dioxide
which induced hallucinations
yep the guy who's so cool under pressure
that he falls asleep durin

claims.rationale: Cooper genuinely flew Mercury-Atlas 9 / Faith 7 in May 1963. There is post-flight commentary attributed to Cooper about anomalous objects, though the most-cited UFO-related stories (the 1957 Edwards saucer film) come from his post-retirement testimony rather than his Mercury flight. The specific 'green glowing object during Mercury 9 confirmed by ground crew + tracking station' is hard to substantiate from contemporary mission records; NASA's published technical debrief does not feature such an entry. Cooper's later UFO advocacy is real; the in-mission specifics are uncertain.

claims.text: After retirement Gordon Cooper testified — including before the United Nations — that during his earlier service as a test pilot at Edwards Air Force Base he saw silent saucer-shaped craft landing on retractable gear, that hundreds of similar reports were confirmed by radar, and that the U.S. government was actively covering up information about UFOs.

evidence.quote:
st pilot at
edwards air force base he saw strange
saucer-shaped craft and the saucers made
no sound as they landed on retractable
gear and cooper said that even the most
fearless test pilots were shaken by the
things they saw at edwards and right up
until his death cooper claimed that the
u.s government was definitely covering
up information about ufos
and yo

he can recall hundreds of
reports made by fellow pilots that were
confirmed by radar cooper even testified
before the united nations about this now
sightings of the black knight satellite
piled up over the years it became
increasingly difficult to deny that
something was up

claims.rationale: Cooper did write a 1978 letter to the UN urging investigation of UFO phenomena and made repeated public statements including a 1995 interview reaffirming his belief in alien visitation. The Edwards 1957 saucer story is well-attested as Cooper's own testimony but the alleged film was never produced. So the meta-claim 'Cooper publicly testified to these things' is solidly true; the truthfulness of the saucer encounters themselves is what's contested.

claims.text: In a 1973 Spaceflight magazine article, Scottish researcher Duncan Lunan claimed he had decoded long-delayed radio echoes into a star map showing the constellation Boötes — and after correcting for 13,000 years of axial procession, concluded the Black Knight satellite is a probe from a planet orbiting Epsilon Boötis that has been here for thirteen thousand years.

evidence.quote:
rticle in spaceflight
magazine duncan london said he had
identified a hidden radio message sent
by the black knight satellite london had
gone back to the lde data and noticed
variations that might indicate a code so
he put the data on a graph on one axis
was the time delay between echoes on the
other axis the position of each echo in
the sequence and plotting these points
didn't create any recognizable patter

arrival according to the map it's been
here for thirteen thousand years
london said that the diagrams were so
clear that they could be interpreted as
a message in standard english the black
knight message says our home is epsilon
which is a double star we live on the
sixth plan

claims.rationale: Duncan Lunan really did publish 'Space Probe From Epsilon Boötis?' in Spaceflight (1973) proposing this exact decoding. The historical claim that he made the proposal is solid. But Lunan himself, as the host accurately notes minutes later, retracted the specific decoding in 1976 acknowledging methodology errors; SETI experts have long classified it as a flawed pattern-fit. directTruth low because the underlying decoding doesn't withstand scrutiny.

claims.text: On December 11, 1998 during STS-88 — the first Space Shuttle mission to assemble the International Space Station — NASA released a series of photos from Endeavour showing what appears to be a craft in orbit around Earth, providing what the host calls long-awaited proof of the Black Knight satellite.

evidence.quote:
photo
on december 4th 1998 the space shuttle
endeavour embarked on mission sts-88 the
first shuttle mission to the
international space station the primary
purpose of this mission was to deliver
and install a piece of equipment called
the unity node unity connects the
russian and american segments together
where the crew can share meals and hang
out but that wasn't the most notable
event of the mission on december 11th a
series of photos were taken and released
by nasa and they show what appears to be
some type of craft in orbit around the
earth

claims.rationale: The historical bones are accurate: STS-88 (Dec 4-15 1998) did install Unity, was the first ISS-assembly mission, and NASA's image archive does contain photos from that EVA showing a dark/shiny floating object. The 'proof of Black Knight' framing is the host's interpretation — those images are the genesis of the modern Black Knight meme. The host walks back this interpretation in the verdict, citing NASA's thermal-blanket explanation.

claims.text: The Tesla/Marconi radio pulses were almost certainly natural pulsars (not officially discovered until 1968), Lunan himself walked back the Boötes star-map decoding for lack of scientific evidence, and the famous 1998 STS-88 'Black Knight' photographs are NASA's documentation of a thermal cover that drifted away during a spacewalk.

evidence.quote:
nto one so let's unravel
the truth throw away what's false and
see what's left so tesla and marconi did
hear radio pulses but they weren't
aliens they were pulsars and pulsars
weren't discovered officially until 1968
so they would have definitely soun

ar maps
it doesn't hold up the scrutiny he later
walked the claims back saying there
wasn't scientific evidence he was just
deducing what he thought the signals
could mean but since then he's revised
his opinion once again and thinks
epsilon bowotis is special booties
now the pictures from nasa are amazing
the official explanation is that du

ring
a spacewalk an astronaut was installing
a thermal cover the cover got away from
him and ended up floating away the
pictures were taken to document this a
space blanket that's what they're going
with well that's the official
explanation and in some of the photos it
does look like a flat shiny object but
in other photos
it looks pretty solid all right

claims.rationale: Pulsars were indeed officially discovered by Bell-Burnell/Hewish in 1967 and announced 1968; periodic radio noise from Tesla's era is consistent with natural sources later identified. Lunan published a public retraction of the Boötes decoding in Spaceflight in 1976. The STS-88 thermal blanket explanation is documented in NASA's mission imagery catalog (the object is a discarded thermal cover from EVA #1, photographed for telemetry purposes). The host's three-part debunk lines up with the actual record.

dependencies.rationale: Reframes Tesla's 1899 'artificial signal' as natural pulsar emission.
Notes Lunan retracted his own decoding.
Identifies the STS-88 'craft' as a discarded thermal blanket.

claims.text: The 1960 'mystery satellite' the U.S. government later attributed to a malfunctioning Discoverer V capsule is suspicious because the news report referenced a 1959 sighting and Discoverer was itself a public cover story for the classified Corona spy-satellite program — so the official rebuttal cannot be taken at face value.

evidence.quote:
s a
newspaper report of an object spotted in
1960. the us government said this was
actually the discoverer 5 capsule that
got off course
fine
why was it moving in retrograde when
satellites didn't do that the government
said that it malfunctioned and the
thrusters sent it in the wrong direction
okay if that's true and it was the
discoverer 5 which was entered into the
database in 1960
how does that explain that even though
the news report was from 1960 it was
actually referring to a sighting earlier
in 1959
well the government said that someone
accidentally entered the wrong date i

that all of this
was a lie anyway the military was using
discoverer as a cover story for the
corona project a spy satellite program
it kept that information classified for
years so let's assume that sighting is
debunked what about all the other
evidence

claims.rationale: Discoverer was indeed a publicly-acknowledged front for the Corona reconnaissance satellite program — declassified in 1995. So the government's 'cover story' history on this exact program is real and documented. The retrograde-orbit puzzle for Discoverer V also has a real basis (its Agena upper stage drifted unexpectedly). The host's epistemic point — that an institution caught lying about Discoverer once isn't a clean narrator — is reasonable, even if the underlying object was indeed a Corona-related artifact rather than aliens.

dependencies.rationale: Provides the host's reasoning that the official rebuttal of the 1960 sighting is itself unreliable.

claims.text: Even after debunking the radio-signal and STS-88 photo evidence, AJ believes the eyewitness reports from astronauts and astronomers who risked their reputations to speak out are credible — concluding there is more to the Black Knight story than the official record admits.

evidence.quote:
knight satellite no do i believe
eyewitness reports from astronauts and
astronomers who put their reputations in
jeopardy by speaking out
yes i do there is clearly more to the
story that we're not being told and
because you're watching this right now
i know you care about the truth
so you have to keep asking questions you
have to keep the pressure on and those
in power will try to dismiss you they
will not take you seriously but that too
is a lie those in power take you very
seriously

claims.rationale: Pure expression of host stance/credo. Not a falsifiable empirical claim — it's an evidentiary preference (weight eyewitness astronaut testimony over institutional explanation). directTruth omitted because what's being asserted is a meta-judgment about credibility, not a checkable proposition.

dependencies.rationale: Cooper testimony is the canonical example of the 'astronaut who put their reputation in jeopardy'.
Vallée is the canonical example of the 'astronomer who spoke out'.
